concepts.name: Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: A step-by-step procedure for solving a problem or accomplishing a task.

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the amount of time an algorithm takes to run as a function of the input size.

concepts.name: Input Size
concepts.short_definition: The number of elements or data points that an algorithm processes.

concepts.name: Asymptotic Analysis
concepts.short_definition: Analyzing the performance of an algorithm as the input size approaches infinity.

concepts.name: Big O Notation
concepts.short_definition: A mathematical notation used to describe the limiting behavior of a function when the argument tends towards a particular value or infinity.

concepts.name: Big Omega Notation
concepts.short_definition: A mathematical notation used to describe the lower bound of an algorithm's running time.

concepts.name: Big Theta Notation
concepts.short_definition: A mathematical notation used to describe both the lower and upper bound of an algorithm's running time.

concepts.name: Constant Time
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm whose execution time is independent of the input size.

concepts.name: Logarithmic Time
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm whose execution time grows logarithmically with the input size.

concepts.name: Linear Time
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm whose execution time grows linearly with the input size.

concepts.name: Linearithmic Time
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm whose execution time grows by a factor of n multiplied by the logarithm of n.

concepts.name: Quadratic Time
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm whose execution time grows by the square of the input size.

concepts.name: Cubic Time
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm whose execution time grows by the cube of the input size.

concepts.name: Polynomial Time
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm whose execution time is bounded by a polynomial function of the input size.

concepts.name: Exponential Time
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm whose execution time grows exponentially with the input size.

concepts.name: Factorial Time
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm whose execution time grows by the factorial of the input size.

concepts.name: Worst-Case Time
concepts.short_definition: The maximum amount of time an algorithm can take to run for a given input size.

concepts.name: Best-Case Time
concepts.short_definition: The minimum amount of time an algorithm can take to run for a given input size.

concepts.name: Average-Case Time
concepts.short_definition: The average amount of time an algorithm takes to run over all possible inputs of a given size.

concepts.name: Recursive Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm that calls itself to solve smaller instances of the same problem.

concepts.name: Recurrence Relation
concepts.short_definition: An equation that recursively defines a sequence or function.

concepts.name: Master Theorem
concepts.short_definition: A theorem used to find the asymptotic behavior of recurrence relations.

concepts.name: Substitution Method
concepts.short_definition: A method for solving recurrence relations by guessing a solution and proving it by induction.

concepts.name: Recursion Tree Method
concepts.short_definition: A method for solving recurrence relations by visualizing the recursive calls as a tree.

concepts.name: Amortized Analysis
concepts.short_definition: Analyzing the average performance of an operation over a sequence of operations.

concepts.name: Space Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the amount of memory an algorithm uses as a function of the input size.

concepts.name: Auxiliary Space
concepts.short_definition: The extra memory used by an algorithm, not including the input.

concepts.name: In-place Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm that uses a constant amount of auxiliary space.

concepts.name: Data Structure
concepts.short_definition: A particular way of organizing and storing data in a computer so that it can be accessed and modified efficiently.

concepts.name: Array
concepts.short_definition: A data structure that stores a collection of elements of the same type in contiguous memory locations.

concepts.name: Linked List
concepts.short_definition: A linear data structure where elements are not stored at contiguous memory locations.

concepts.name: Stack
concepts.short_definition: A linear data structure that follows the Last-In, First-Out (LIFO) principle.

concepts.name: Queue
concepts.short_definition: A linear data structure that follows the First-In, First-Out (FIFO) principle.

concepts.name: Tree
concepts.short_definition: A hierarchical data structure consisting of nodes connected by edges.

concepts.name: Graph
concepts.short_definition: A non-linear data structure consisting of nodes (vertices) and edges.

concepts.name: Hash Table
concepts.short_definition: A data structure that implements an associative array abstract data type, mapping keys to values.

concepts.name: Sorting Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm that puts elements of a list into a certain order.

concepts.name: Searching Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm for finding an item in a list of items.

concepts.name: Big Data
concepts.short_definition: Extremely large data sets that may be analyzed computationally to reveal patterns, trends, and associations.
